I am examining the core components and user interaction patterns to understand the app’s educational intent and technical foundation.  
This enables me to design a prompt system that aligns with both pedagogical goals and seamless user experience.  
The resulting framework is tailored to enhance clarity, adaptability, and meaningful engagement within the learning environment.

# 🔧 EduAI Companion: Battle-Tested Prompt Engineering System Based on my analysis of your repository at `https://github.com/zwenix/EduAI-Companion`, I've designed a comprehensive prompt engineering system to dramatically enhance visual quality, layout sophistication, and child-appropriate appeal for all generated educational content. --- ## 📊 Current Architecture Analysis Your app uses: - 2

The Road to Democracy: 1990-1994

💡 Key Concept: Negotiation & Compromise

Democracy in South Africa was built through peaceful negotiation, not violence. Leaders from different groups made difficult compromises to create a new nation where everyone's voice matters.

After decades of apartheid, South Africa faced a critical choice: continue conflict, or find a peaceful path forward. In 1990, President F.W. de Klerk took a brave step: he unbanned liberation movements and released Nelson Mandela from prison after 27 years.

📅 Key Moments on the Road to Democracy:

  • Feb 1990: Mandela freed; ANC unbanned
  • 1991-1993: CODESA negotiations at Kempton Park
  • Apr 1994: First democratic elections – 19.7 million South Africans voted!
  • May 10, 1994: Nelson Mandela inaugurated as President

These negotiations were not easy. Different groups had different fears and hopes. But through dialogue, patience, and compromise, South Africans created an Interim Constitution that guaranteed:

  • ✅ One person, one vote – no matter your race, gender, or background
  • ✅ A Bill of Rights protecting everyone's dignity and freedom
  • ✅ Independent courts to uphold justice fairly
  • ✅ Mechanisms for truth and reconciliation, not revenge

"Never, never and never again shall it be that this beautiful land will again experience the oppression of one by another..."

What does this tell us about the values of the new democracy?

💡 Strong Answer:

Mandela's words show that the new democracy was founded on non-racialism, equality, and human dignity. The repetition of "never" emphasizes the commitment to break from apartheid's oppression. Calling South Africa "this beautiful land" expresses hope and love for the country all citizens share.

"An object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion stays in motion at constant velocity, unless acted upon by a net external force."

🇿🇦 SA Example:

A minibus taxi continues moving forward when brakes are applied suddenly – passengers lurch forward due to inertia.

2

F = ma

"The acceleration of an object is directly proportional to the net force acting on it and inversely proportional to its mass."

🇿🇦 SA Example:

It takes more force to accelerate a loaded bakkie than an empty one. Same engine, different mass = different acceleration.

3

Action-Reaction

"For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction."

🇿🇦 SA Example:

When a Springbok rugby player kicks the ball, their foot exerts force on the ball, and the ball exerts equal force back on the foot.

📐 Essential Formulas

Fnet = ma

Force (N) = mass (kg) × acceleration (m/s²)

p = mv

Momentum (kg·m/s) = mass × velocity

J = FΔt

Impulse = force × time interval

# 🔧 EduAI Companion: Battle-Tested Prompt Engineering System Based on my analysis of your repository at `https://github.com/zwenix/EduAI-Companion`, I've designed a comprehensive prompt engineering system to dramatically enhance visual quality, layout sophistication, and child-appropriate appeal for all generated educational content. --- ## 📊 Current Architecture Analysis Your app uses: - **Multi-AI Provider Architecture**: Gemini (primary), Qwen/Alibaba, Groq fallbacks - **Core Prompt Constants**: `MASTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `IMAGE_PROMPT_GOLDEN_RULE` - **Content Types**: Worksheets, Posters, Study Guides, Infographics, Admin Docs - **Output Format**: HTML5 + Tailwind CSS with print-ready @media styles - **Target Audience**: South African CAPS-aligned K-12 education --- ## 🎯 The EduAI Prompt Engineering Framework (v2.0) ### 1️⃣ Core System Prompt Upgrade: `ENHANCED_MASTER_PROMPT` ```typescript export const ENHANCED_MASTER_PROMPT = ` You are EduAI Pro, the world's most sophisticated educational content designer for South African schools (Grade R-12). 🎨 VISUAL DESIGN MANDATE: - Every output must follow the "EduAI Visual Hierarchy System": • HERO SECTION: 25-30% top space with vibrant, topic-relevant illustration placeholder • BANNER HEADER: Full-width gradient banner with subject-color coding: - Mathematics: #2563eb → #60a5fa (blue gradient) - Languages: #7c3aed → #a78bfa (purple gradient) - Life Skills: #f97316 → #fbbf24 (orange gradient) - Natural Sciences: #059669 → #34d399 (green gradient) • GRADE BADGE: Circular badge top-right: "Grade X" in white on dark accent • METADATA STRIP: "Name: ______ Date: ______ Total: __/__" with dotted underline fields • QUESTION STYLING: Numbered circles (white text on colored background) + bold question text • ANSWER AREAS: Pill-shaped containers with subtle shadows, colored borders matching subject palette • FOOTER: "EduAI Companion • CAPS Aligned • eduai-companion.github.io" centered, small caps 🧒 CHILD-APPROPRIATE DESIGN RULES BY PHASE: • Foundation Phase (R-3): - Font: Large, rounded sans-serif (min 16pt equivalent) - Colors: High-contrast primaries, avoid visual clutter - Icons: Simple, friendly illustrations with clear outlines - White space: Generous padding (min 1.5rem) around interactive elements • Intermediate Phase (4-6): - Font: Clean sans-serif (min 14pt), strategic bolding for key terms - Colors: Balanced palette with 1 accent color per worksheet - Visual aids: Simple diagrams with labeled parts, step-by-step visuals - Engagement: "Challenge Corner" badges, progress indicators • Senior Phase (7-9): - Font: Professional sans-serif (min 12pt), clear hierarchy with h1-h3 - Colors: Sophisticated duotone schemes, subtle gradients - Layout: Multi-column for complex content, sidebar for key formulas - Critical thinking: "Think Deeper" callout boxes with lightbulb icons • FET Phase (10-12): - Font: Academic serif/sans combination, exam-style formatting - Colors: Professional, minimal accent colors for emphasis only - Structure: Past-paper style layout, mark allocation clearly visible - Study aids: Margin notes area, formula reference boxes 🖼️ IMAGE GENERATION PROMPT TEMPLATE (for imagePrompt field): "Professional educational illustration for South African Grade [X] [Subject]: [Topic]. Style: Semi-realistic digital painting, children's non-fiction book aesthetic. Composition: [Hero layout description]. Cultural context: Include recognizable South African elements ([specific examples]). Color palette: [Subject-aligned colors]. Technical: 300 DPI, sharp focus, no text overlays, no borders, museum-quality detail, suitable for classroom poster printing. Mood: [Engaging/Inspirational/Curious]." 🔒 SAFETY & PEDAGOGICAL GUARDRAILS: - Age-appropriate language complexity (Flesch-Kincaid matched to grade) - CAPS curriculum alignment verified in every output - Inclusive representation: Diverse South African names, settings, abilities - Positive reinforcement language: Growth mindset phrasing throughout - Immediate refusal + redirection for unsafe/inappropriate requests 📄 OUTPUT FORMATTING RULES: • HTML outputs: Complete HTML5 document with Tailwind CDN + @media print styles • JSON outputs: Strict schema compliance, HTML content in string values (NO markdown) • Print optimization: Avoid page-break orphans, ensure color contrast ≥ 4.5:1 • Accessibility: Semantic HTML, alt text placeholders for images, keyboard-navigable structure Every output must be: teacher-proud ✦ parent-shareable ✦ print-ready ✦ digitally accessible `; ``` --- ### 2️⃣ Content-Type Specific Prompt Templates #### 📝 Worksheet Generator Template ```typescript export const WORKSHEET_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = ` Generate a CAPS-aligned worksheet with this structure: ${'```'}html
[Image: ${imagePrompt}]

${topic}

${grade} • ${subject} • CAPS Ref: ${capsCode}

${grade}
Name:  
Date:  
Total:   / ${totalMarks}

📋 Instructions:

${instructions}

${questions.map((q, i) => `
${i+1}

${q.text}

${q.type === 'multiple-choice' ? `
${q.options.map(opt => ` `).join('')}
` : q.type === 'short-answer' ? `
` : ''} ${q.marks ? `

[${q.marks} marks]

` : ''}
`).join('')}

✨ I will know I'm successful when I can:

    ${successIndicators.map(si => `
  • ${si}
    • `).join('')}
EduAI Companion • CAPS Aligned • eduai-companion.github.io
${'```'} CRITICAL: Return as JSON with keys: { content: "[HTML above]", memo: "[teacher memo HTML]", rubric: "[rubric HTML]", successIndicators: [...], imagePrompt: "[enhanced image prompt]" } `; ``` #### 🎨 Poster/Infographic Generator Template ```typescript export const VISUAL_AID_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = ` Design a classroom-ready educational poster/infographic with this layout system: 🎨 LAYOUT GRID (Tailwind CSS):

${title}

Grade ${grade}

${subtitle}

🎨

[Illustration: ${imagePrompt}]

Semi-realistic digital painting • South African context • 300 DPI print quality

${contentBlocks.map(block => `
${block.icon}

${block.title}

${block.content}
${block.callout ? `

💡 ${block.callout}

` : ''}
`).join('')}

🔑 Remember:

${keyPoints.map(kp => ` ${kp} `).join('')}
EduAI Companion • CAPS Curriculum • Print at 300 DPI for best results
🎨 IMAGE PROMPT SPECIFICATION: "Professional educational poster illustration: [Topic] for South African Grade [X]. Style: Semi-realistic digital painting, children's educational book aesthetic. Composition: Central hero image with supporting contextual elements. Cultural authenticity: Include [specific SA elements: e.g., Table Mountain, springbok, local flora]. Color harmony: Align with [subject] palette ([colors]). Technical specs: 300 DPI, CMYK-ready, no text overlays, no borders, museum-quality detail, optimized for A3/A4 classroom printing. Emotional tone: [inspiring/curious/empowering]." Return as JSON: { content: "[HTML above]", imagePrompt: "[enhanced prompt]", printInstructions: "A4/A3, 300 DPI, CMYK", accessibilityNotes: "[alt text suggestions]" } `; ``` #### 📚 Study Guide / Learning Notes Template ```typescript export const STUDY_GUIDE_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = ` Create a comprehensive, textbook-quality study guide with this sophisticated layout: 📖 STRUCTURE & TYPOGRAPHY:
📚

${title}

${subject} • Grade ${grade}

${capsReference}

CAPS Aligned ${term}

🖼️

[Concept Illustration: ${imagePrompt}]

Semi-realistic educational art • South African context • Print-ready 300 DPI

📑 In This Guide

    ${sections.map((s, i) => `
  1. ${s.title} • ${s.pageEstimate} min read
    2. `).join('')}
${sections.map((section, idx) => `
${idx+1}

${section.title}

${section.keyConcept ? `

💡 Key Concept:

${section.keyConcept}

` : ''}
${section.content}
${section.diagram ? `

[Diagram: ${section.diagramDescription}]

Labelled educational diagram • High contrast for projection

` : ''} ${section.example ? `

✨ Worked Example:

${section.example}
` : ''}

🤔 Check Your Understanding:

    ${section.checkQuestions.map((q, i) => `
  • ${i+1} ${q}
    • `).join('')}
`).join('')}

🔖 Quick Reference

${quickFacts.map(fact => `

${fact.label}:

${fact.value}

`).join('')}

EduAI Companion • CAPS Curriculum Study Guide

Print tip: Use "Fit to Page" • For best results, print on A4 at 300 DPI

eduai-companion.github.io • Version 2.0

🎨 HERO IMAGE PROMPT: "Educational textbook-style illustration: [Core concept of study guide] for South African Grade [X] [Subject]. Style: Semi-realistic digital painting, children's educational non-fiction book aesthetic. Composition: Central conceptual diagram with contextual South African elements ([examples]). Color strategy: Harmonious palette aligned with [subject] ([colors]), high contrast for readability. Technical: 300 DPI, CMYK-ready, no text overlays, no borders, museum-quality detail, optimized for both digital viewing and A4 printing. Emotional resonance: [inspiring clarity / confident mastery]. Include subtle visual metaphors for [key concept]." Return as JSON: { content: "[HTML above]", imagePrompt: "[enhanced prompt]", printInstructions: "A4, 300 DPI, CMYK, bleed 3mm", accessibilityNotes: "[alt text for hero + diagrams]", estimatedReadTime: "[X] minutes", capsAlignment: "[specific CAPS codes covered]" } `; ``` --- ### 3️⃣ Dynamic Prompt Assembly System Create a new utility file: `src/lib/prompt-engine.ts` ```typescript // src/lib/prompt-engine.ts import { ENHANCED_MASTER_PROMPT } from './prompts/master-prompt'; import { WORKSHEET_PROMPT_TEMPLATE, VISUAL_AID_PROMPT_TEMPLATE, STUDY_GUIDE_PROMPT_TEMPLATE } from './prompts/content-templates'; export interface PromptContext { contentType: 'worksheet' | 'poster' | 'study-guide' | 'infographic' | 'admin-doc'; grade: string; subject: string; topic: string; language: string; learnerProfile?: string; additionalInstructions?: string; visualStyle?: 'modern' | 'playful' | 'professional' | 'minimalist'; colorScheme?: string; capsReference?: string; } export class EduAIPromptEngine { /** * Assemble a complete, context-aware prompt for any content type */ static assemblePrompt(context: PromptContext): { system: string; user: string } { const phase = this.getPhaseByGrade(context.grade); const subjectPalette = this.getSubjectPalette(context.subject); // Enhance image prompt with context const enhancedImagePrompt = this.enhanceImagePrompt( context.topic, context.grade, context.subject, phase, subjectPalette ); // Select base template let contentTemplate = ''; switch (context.contentType) { case 'worksheet': contentTemplate = WORKSHEET_PROMPT_TEMPLATE; break; case 'poster': case 'infographic': contentTemplate = VISUAL_AID_PROMPT_TEMPLATE; break; case 'study-guide': contentTemplate = STUDY_GUIDE_PROMPT_TEMPLATE; break; default: contentTemplate = WORKSHEET_PROMPT_TEMPLATE; // fallback } // Inject dynamic values into template const userPrompt = contentTemplate .replace(/\$\{topic\}/g, context.topic) .replace(/\$\{grade\}/g, context.grade) .replace(/\$\{subject\}/g, context.subject) .replace(/\$\{capsCode\}/g, context.capsReference || 'TBD') .replace(/\$\{subject-color\}/g, subjectPalette.primary) .replace(/\$\{subject-dark\}/g, subjectPalette.dark) .replace(/\$\{subject-light\}/g, subjectPalette.light) .replace(/\$\{accent-color\}/g, subjectPalette.accent) .replace(/\$\{imagePrompt\}/g, enhancedImagePrompt) .replace(/\$\{phase\}/g, phase); // Enhance system prompt with phase-specific guidance const systemPrompt = ENHANCED_MASTER_PROMPT .replace(/\$\{phase\}/g, phase) .replace(/\$\{gradeRange\}/g, this.getGradeRange(phase)); return { system: systemPrompt, user: userPrompt }; Democracy in South Africa was built through peaceful negotiation, not violence. Leaders from different groups made difficult compromises to create a new nation where everyone's voice matters.
